critical_failures: Security: The function appends unescaped content ($before, $after) derived from user-configurable options to RSS feed output without any output escaping. Even though rss_replace_vars() may perform some sanitization, the returned content is concatenated directly into feed output without context-appropriate escaping (e.g., wp_kses_post() or esc_html()), risking injection of arbitrary HTML/JavaScript into RSS feeds consumed by feed readers.
From src/php-toolkit/ internal library — not WordPress plugin patterns, not assessable as WordPress training data
camelCase method naming violates WPCS (WordPress requires snake_case)

code:

critical_failures: Missing PHPDoc block with @since, @return, and @global tags for a public API function that uses globals

Relies on mutable global state ($ossdl_off_blog_url, $ossdl_off_cdn_url) populated via side-effect call to scossdl_off_get_options() rather than returning structured data — poor encapsulation and testability
Function name 'do_scossdl_off_ob_start' does not clearly follow WordPress naming conventions and the 'do_' prefix is non-standard for a registration function
Moodle code, not WordPress - excluded from training data

PHPDoc block uses non-standard @type tag instead of proper @method or missing @since on correct line; @param is missing type hint documentation (uses '(object)' as literal text instead of proper PHP type); @return documents 'n/a' instead of void
Function is not prefixed — bare method name 'customize_preview_init' in a class context is acceptable, but PHPDoc is non-conformant for WordPress Coding Standards

No capability or nonce verification before acting on customizer data — while customizer context implies some authentication, there is no explicit check that the current user has 'customize' capability before populating preview_values/preview_fields
Indentation uses tabs but is inconsistently aligned (extra leading whitespace suggests copy-paste formatting issues with mixed indentation levels)
get_taxonomy() can return false if the taxonomy does not exist; accessing ->labels->singular_name on a false value will cause a fatal PHP error — no null/false check before property access

The returned label data is derived from taxonomy object properties which may contain unescaped content; callers using this in HTML output have no escape contract established, and the string concatenation ' (' . $taxonomy . ')' is unescaped

code: /** * Displaying the option fields in custom field settings page. * * @param int $group_key Group key. * @param int $field_key Field key. */ function display_field_options( $group_key, $field_key ) { $this->display_label_option( $group_key, $field_key ); $this->display_name_option( $group_key, $field_key ); ?> true, ), 'objects' ); unset( $post_types['attachment'] ); unset( $post_types['wp_block'] ); unset( $post_types[ SCF_Config::NAME ] ); ?> $post_type_object ) : ?> get( 'post-type' ); ?> checked="checked" />labels->singular_name ); ?>
